title: Stride-8 fine-layout tap: the field scorer reads an 8-m-resolution mid-trunk snapshot, int8-paid
hypothesis: The binding constraint is the information content of the head's readout, not trunk capacity, optimization, or memorization — this is exp 26's own pre-registered diagnostic fork (a), now demonstrated by the joint record: exp 20 removed memorization (train-split and eval-split medians are EQUAL), exp 25 trained to a genuine loss plateau and moved the metric only 5.2%, and exps 26+28 tripled trunk depth/channels and made things WORSE at the designed operating point (1057.68 m vs 797.91 m best). Per the plateau rule this iteration leaves the capacity-scaling mechanism entirely and attacks the bottleneck those refutations jointly point at. The field scorer today sees only a 560-d summary of the crop: a 48-d GAP plus a 512-d layout code squeezed from the stride-16 8x8 grid — every layout cell aggregates a 16x16 m patch, so NOTHING finer than 16 m granularity survives to the 1024-way cell scorer, while the ~215 m map cells it must discriminate differ precisely in sub-16-m arrangement (building outlines, road widths, lot patterns) that the 1 m/px input plainly contains. Two signatures say the discarded fine scale is what's missing: midday errors equal night errors in every area (656-839 m metros) even though midday crops carry clean 1-m detail — if the readout could use it, daytime would lead; and rural prignitz (distinctive macro-structure, low self-similarity) beats all three self-similar metros (449-539 m), the signature of lookalike-cell confusion, not lighting. The change adds the one axis never scaled: SPATIAL resolution of the readout. The trunk's stride-8 activation (features block 3, 16x16x24) is tapped, squeezed by a 1x1 conv to 4 channels, and flattened into a 1,024-d fine layout code concatenated into the bright field head's input (560 -> 1,584). Trunk computation, pretrained weights, C4 vote, decode, confidence, and training are all byte-identical — unlike refuted exps 26/28, no capacity is added to the encoder; the head is simply allowed to SEE a scale the encoder already computes and then throws away. The enlarged FC heads fly as int8 (quantize_dynamic on MatMul/Gemm only), which runs/_probe_iter6_quant2.py proved metrically inert on this exact head family (~7 m median decode shift, no ConvInteger, latency fine): estimated export ~2.6 MB against the 4 MiB gate.
method: model/model.py only. (1) TinyLocNet.__init__: probe the stride-8 tap shape by running features[:4] on a zero tensor (gives 24x16x16); add self.fine_squeeze = nn.Conv2d(24, 4, 1); widen loc_logits input from feat_ch + layout_ch*8*8 = 560 to feat_ch + layout_ch*8*8 + 4*16*16 = 1584. (2) forward: run the trunk as an enumerated loop over self.features, capturing the activation after block index 3 as the tap; fine4 = self.fine_squeeze(tap).flatten(1); bright head becomes self.loc_logits(torch.cat([f4, layout4, fine4], dim=1)). Dark head, gate, conf head, C4 stack, decode, loss_fn all untouched. (3) export_onnx: export fp32 to <stem>_fp32_debug.onnx, then onnxruntime.quantization.quantize_dynamic(QInt8, op_types_to_quantize=['MatMul','Gemm']) to the contract path <out>/models/<area>.onnx; assert no ConvInteger node and size <= 4 MiB. model/train.py unchanged.
expected_outcome: If the readout is fine-detail starved, the worst-case median drops from 797.91 m to ~650-750 m (-6% to -19%), with gains LARGEST in the daytime buckets (morning/midday/afternoon) and the three metro areas — the cells where 1-m detail is cleanest and lookalike aliasing is worst; night cells improve least (sensor noise masks stride-8 texture). Kept iff < 797.91 m. Diagnostic forks if refuted: (a) metric within ±3% of 797.91 with healthy losses — the readout's information width is exonerated, leaving rotation residual (up to ±45° after the C4 vote) and intrinsic 128-m-footprint ambiguity as the demonstrated next bottlenecks; (b) int8 artifact scores clearly worse than its saved <area>_fp32_debug.onnx — a quantization surprise (probe predicts ~7 m, i.e. none); (c) training loss does not drop below exp 25's plateau (berlin 4.78) — the fine tap carries no transferable location signal under the relight sim's statistics.
eli5: Before guessing where it is, the model boils each camera frame down to a short summary — like describing a neighborhood in 560 numbers, where each number can only talk about 16-metre-wide squares. At that coarseness, two similar districts blur together, and our last three experiments proved that a bigger brain and longer studying don't help — the tell-apart detail was already thrown away before the guessing stage ever saw it. This experiment keeps everything else identical but lets the guesser also look at a sharper, earlier snapshot from inside the network, where building edges and narrow roads are still visible at roughly 8-metre precision. To keep the file small enough for the drone's tiny computer, the guesser's bulky lookup tables are stored as compact 1-byte numbers — a trick we measured beforehand to be harmless.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns (exact pixel re-arrangements, no blur) and all four copies travel through the same network side by side

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small, first 9 blocks — untouched this round; it shrinks the image in steps and its final grid is 8×8

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: alongside the usual 512-number map of what sits where (drawn from the final 8×8 grid, 16-m squares) and the 48-number texture average, a second, sharper snapshot is now taken HALFWAY through the extractor — before the image shrinks past 16×16 — and condensed into 1,024 extra numbers that record where fine details like building edges and narrow roads sit, at about 8-metre precision

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: a tiny network reads the crop's own average brightness together with the 48-number texture summary and outputs one number: how night-like is this shot?

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: two scorers read each of the four turned copies and the gate blends their per-cell guesses into one voted heat map; the main scorer's connection table grows to read the new sharper snapshot (1,584 inputs instead of 560), and the bulky tables fly as compact 1-byte integers — a storage trick measured beforehand to cost only ~7 m of wobble

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the heat map's contrast is turned up first (peaks boosted, faint background suppressed), then the answer is the balance point of the sharpened map — committing to the strongest hotspot instead of averaging every faint guess across the whole city

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: the model inspects the shape of its own heat map and says 'no fix' below a tuned bar — tuned as before on places it visited during training

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: unchanged — same position and confidence grading as before

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: unchanged — a fresh 6,000-place draw per lighting condition every pass, each place seen at a fresh heading and one of three relight re-renders

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: unchanged — 24 fresh-draw passes with the cosine glide kept from round 5

implementation_brief: Edit model/model.py ONLY; model/train.py, pipeline/*, and all frozen files stay byte-identical. FIXED CONTRACTS TO PRESERVE: train.py's CLI (--area --out-dir --data-dir --epochs --max-crops-per-bucket --seed) is untouched because train.py is untouched; the ONNX contract from model/model.py's docstring — train.py exports one model per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx taking a 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1] named 'frame' and returning [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords and conf in [0,1] — and the frozen deployment gates in pipeline/score.py (file <= 4 MiB, single-thread host latency <= 250 ms). build_model(), loss_fn signature, estimate_position, GRID_K=32, TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS, DECODE_BETA, GOOD_ERR_UV, the C4 _c4_stack order, and the pretrained load (mnv3s_features8.pt, strict=True) all stay exactly as they are. CHANGE 1 — TinyLocNet.__init__: after the existing zero-tensor probe of self.features (feat_ch=48, fmap_hw=8), add a second probe: mid = self.features[:4](torch.zeros(1, 3, 128, 128)) inside the same torch.no_grad() block; read mid_ch = mid.shape[1] (24) and mid_hw = mid.shape[2] (16). Define TAP_IDX = 3 as a module-level constant with a one-line comment (stride-8 tap: output of MobileNetV3-Small block 3, 16x16x24 on a 128^2 input). Add self.fine_squeeze = nn.Conv2d(mid_ch, 4, 1). Change self.loc_logits to nn.Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch * fmap_hw * fmap_hw + 4 * mid_hw * mid_hw, grid_k * grid_k) — i.e. 1584 -> 1024. dark_logits, gate, conf_head, buffers unchanged. CHANGE 2 — TinyLocNet.forward: replace the single call fmap = self.features((x4 - self.norm_mean) / self.norm_std) with an enumerated loop: h = (x4 - self.norm_mean) / self.norm_std; then for i, blk in enumerate(self.features): h = blk(h); if i == TAP_IDX: tap = h. After the loop fmap = h. Compute fine4 = self.fine_squeeze(tap).flatten(1) and change ONLY the bright head input: bright_logits4 = self.loc_logits(torch.cat([f4, layout4, fine4], dim=1)). Everything else in forward — lum, _c4_stack, f4, layout4, dark_logits4, gate, logit averaging, both softmaxes, conf head, output cat, return_logits — is byte-identical. The tap is computed per turned copy inside the C4 stack (batch 4N), exactly like layout4; do not de-duplicate it. CHANGE 3 — export_onnx(model, path): keep the signature. Export fp32 with the existing torch.onnx.export call but to a sibling debug path: p = Path(path); fp32_path = p.with_name(p.stem + '_fp32_debug.onnx'). Then: from onnxruntime.quantization import quantize_dynamic, QuantType; quantize_dynamic(fp32_path, path, weight_type=QuantType.QInt8, op_types_to_quantize=['MatMul', 'Gemm']). This is the exact recipe runs/_probe_iter6_quant2.py validated (convs stay fp32; ~7 m decode shift). After quantizing, load the result with onnx.load and assert (a) no node has op_type 'ConvInteger' (the probe-documented fatal op) and (b) Path(path).stat().st_size <= 4*1024*1024; raise RuntimeError on either failure — never fall back to shipping the fp32 file, it is ~7.5 MB and fails the frozen gate. Leave the _fp32_debug.onnx file in place in <out>/models/ for post-hoc forensics (precedent: exp 26); pipeline/score.py only reads <area>.onnx so the debug file is inert. CHANGE 4 — docstring: append a short 'Exp 29' paragraph to the model/model.py module docstring describing the stride-8 fine-layout tap and the int8 FC export, keeping the existing contract wording intact. SANITY NUMBERS the implementer can check: new param count ~1.87M (loc_logits 1584*1024+1024 = 1,623,040; fine_squeeze 24*4+4 = 100; all else as before); fp32 debug export ~7.5 MB; quantized export ~2.5-2.7 MB; latency well under 250 ms (probe measured 4.6 ms on a BIGGER int8 model). Training memory is unchanged (the sampler is untouched); the head params (fine_squeeze included) automatically land in train.py's 1e-3 'head' param group because that group is defined as all-params-not-in-model.features — verify fine_squeeze is NOT under self.features so this holds.
